auth_notes: Authentication typically requires an API Key generated within the Pars Inventory administrative dashboard, passed via the 'Authorization' header as a Bearer token or a custom 'X-API-KEY' header.
rate_limits: Typically 60 requests per minute per API key; subject to specific plan tiers.
gotchas: Documentation is often behind a client login and may not be fully public.
Pagination follows a non-standard offset pattern requiring specific logic in n8n loops.
Data types for stock levels (integers vs floats) can be inconsistent across endpoints.
research_notes: Since no native node exists, the integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. Users will need to manually define the JSON body for POST/PUT requests and handle response parsing. Polling is required for data synchronization as native webhooks are not standard for this platform.
